q3zU7Yy5E5s.root_cause_summary: Both detected stair centroids in scene q3zU7Yy5E5s are inside collision geometry: centroid [-1.30898204, 3.5508982] (selected in candidates 11–14, 16, 18, 19, 20, 22, 23) produces 27 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False then pointnav abort; centroid [-2.10394737, 3.27368421] (selected in candidate_15 via DP10='replace') produces 35 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False then abort. floor_step NEVER resets to 0 in any of the 14 candidates, confirming zero physical floor crossings. Candidate_23's perpendicular advance patch (targeting mL8ThkuaVTM premature frontier exhaustion) had zero effect — steps=418, reinits=2, stair_runs=2, dp7_empty=4/4 remain bit-for-bit identical across all 14 candidates — confirming the failure is exclusively stair centroid unreachability and is fully orthogonal to both mL8ThkuaVTM and qyAac8rV8Zk root causes. Fix requires a Track 2 nav_mesh validity check in ascent_policy.py; all 12 harness DPs are exhausted.
key_evidence: floor_step=161 at step 390 (Mode=look_down, Stair_flag=2); floor_step never resets to 0 across all 14 candidates — no physical floor traverse in any run

qyAac8rV8Zk.root_cause_summary: Two distinct stair centroid failure sub-modes are confirmed across 14 candidates. In candidates 11–14, 16, 18, 19, 20, 22, and 23, centroid [-1.26861111, -8.18583333] exhibits a deflection pattern (min_dis increases 170→177, agent moves away) consistent with a navigation obstacle or disconnected nav_mesh region blocking the approach path. In candidate_15 with DP10='replace', a different centroid [-1.20948276, -8.26961207] exhibits an approach pattern (min_dis decreases 173→167→166→161 over 17 steps) but Reach_stair_centroid remains False throughout, consistent with a centroid geometrically approachable in 2D grid distance but on physically inaccessible collision mesh. Candidate_23's perpendicular advance patch (targeting mL8ThkuaVTM) had zero effect — steps=243, reinits=1, stair_runs=1, dp7_empty=1/1, DTG=3.9575 remain bit-for-bit identical — confirming the root cause is exclusively stair centroid reachability and that this scene's failure class is fully orthogonal to both mL8ThkuaVTM and q3zU7Yy5E5s.
key_evidence: Candidates 11–14, 16, 18, 19, 20, 22, 23: min_dis_to_downstair increases 170→177 over successive approach steps — agent is deflected away from centroid [-1.26861111, -8.18583333], consistent with a disconnected nav_mesh region or navigation obstacle on approach path

candidate_15: different centroid [-1.20948276, -8.26961207]; min_dis DECREASES 173→167→166→161 over 17 consecutive steps — agent successfully approaches centroid geometrically but Reach_stair_centroid remains False throughout; centroid is approachable but physically unreachable (on collision mesh boundary or 3D obstacle surface)
floor_step never resets to 0 in any of the 14 candidates — no physical floor crossing in any run

mL8ThkuaVTM.root_cause_summary: The agent physically climbs stairs at step 120 ('climb stair success!!!!' confirmed in all 14 candidates) but BFS seeding initializes from the narrow stair-landing footprint, producing a deterministic 13-step frontier exhaustion (floor_step=13 at step 133) in every candidate. Candidate_20 (FWD advance, 2 extra reinit cycles, steps=250, DTG=4.713) and candidate_23 (perpendicular LEFT advance, steps=224, DTG=4.661) both converge to the identical dead-end frontier [3.88535534, -0.08535534] via different advance directions, exhausting the direction-of-advance hypothesis space and confirming that ALL lateral directions from the stair landing lead to the same unreachable room. Three tested exploration strategies — no advance (c16–c19, c22: DTG≈4.168–4.180), FWD advance (c20: DTG=4.713), and perpendicular-LEFT advance (c23: DTG=4.661) — all fail. The 13-step exhaustion is a structural fixed point of the stair-landing BFS seed; the toilet-containing room is not accessible from any direction through a landing-anchored frontier set. Full BFS re-seeding from ALL navigable cells on floor 2 (not anchored to the landing footprint) is confirmed as the minimum necessary and only remaining structural fix.
key_evidence: 'climb stair success!!!!' at step 120 with floor_step=0 confirmed in all 14 candidates — physical stair traverse confirmed; 'mapping_floor_confusion' heuristic class is incorrect for this scene

floor_step=13 at step 133, floor_step=0 at step 135, 'Environment 0: In all floors, no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping' at step 148 — byte-identical in candidates 11–19 and c16, c18, c19; identical stair climb sequence reproduced in c22 and c23

candidate_23: steps=224, reinits=3, stair_runs=0, dp7_empty=0/2, DTG=4.661 — perpendicular (TURN_LEFT×3 + MOVE_FORWARD×3) advance fired; 2 LLM calls made (both parsed successfully, 0 empty); DP1 logs show best_frontier=[3.88535534, -0.08535534] continuously across 19+ consecutive selections at decreasing distances 5.3m→2.5m — IDENTICAL dead-end room as c20's FWD advance; toilet not found; 'Environment 0: In all floors, no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping' at step 224

Both c20 (FWD advance, DTG=4.713) and c23 (90°-LEFT advance, DTG=4.661) select identical best_frontier [3.88535534, -0.08535534] and navigate to it at similar distance profiles — confirms the stair-landing BFS-seeded frontier set has exactly one accessible corridor regardless of advance direction; the toilet-containing room is structurally unreachable from the landing footprint via any directional advance

ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: CoT prompt changes in candidates 6/7 had no effect; in c20 and c23, successful LLM calls (dp7_empty=0/2) still fail to direct agent to toilet, confirming prompt quality is not the bottleneck
ruled_out_reasoning.DP7: dp7_empty=0/0 in candidates 11–19 — no LLM calls made before termination; in c20 and c23, dp7_empty=0/2 (both LLM responses parsed successfully) but toilet still not found in either; parse quality is not the bottleneck
ruled_out_reasoning.DP9: candidate_10 changed carrot to 1.2m with no effect; stair already climbs successfully under default carrot
ruled_out_reasoning.DP10: candidate_15 'replace' fusion: identical steps=148, reinits=3, dp7_empty=0/0; candidate_16 reverts to 'default': identical steps=148 — floor-2 frontier pool emptiness is invariant to all three tested fusion modes
ruled_out_reasoning.DP11: value map update weighting cannot generate new frontiers on structurally empty floor
ruled_out_reasoning.DP12: candidate_11 DP12=100: identical 148-step stop; 'all floors explored' termination does not check DP12

highest_leverage_untested_levers: Frontier re-seeding from ALL navigable cells after stair climb (Track 2 / ascent_policy.py): candidate_23 decisively closes the direction-of-advance hypothesis — both FWD (c20) and perpendicular-LEFT (c23) advance from the landing converge to the same dead-end [3.88535534, -0.08535534]; the fix must change WHAT nav_mesh cells are used to seed the frontier BFS, not which direction the agent advances or how many reinit cycles are allowed; full BFS from all navigable cells on floor 2 is the minimum necessary change

Combined 'all floors explored' guard threshold + full BFS re-seeding (Track 2 / ascent_policy.py): the reinit-count extension mechanism (proven functional in c20 and c22) is necessary scaffolding but insufficient alone; the fix must combine (a) a minimum floor_step threshold before declaring a floor exhausted AND (b) BFS re-seeding from all nav_mesh cells at floor initialization rather than the landing footprint

Second staircase detection during extended exploration (Track 2 / ascent_policy.py): candidate_22 (stair_runs=0, dp7_empty=0/0) and candidate_23 (stair_runs=0, dp7_empty=0/2 but no stair re-detection) both confirm that passive stair detection does not fire from the explored landing area and dead-end corridor; if the toilet is on floor 3, second-stair detection must be triggered from a broader frontier set exposed by the full BFS re-seed
open_questions: Candidate_23 answers the direction-of-advance question: both FWD (c20) and 90°-LEFT (c23) lead to [3.88535534, -0.08535534]. Is this dead-end the ONLY room accessible from the stair landing, implying the toilet-containing room is separated by a corridor or doorway that is out of sight from the landing? Full BFS from all nav_mesh cells would determine the connectivity of the entire floor.

Is the toilet on floor 2 (the landed floor, unreachable from the landing corridor) or on a floor 3 requiring a second stair climb? Candidate_23's stair_runs=0 even in the extended phase (with 2 LLM calls) suggests no second staircase was detected from the explored landing area or dead-end room; if the toilet is on floor 3, second-staircase access from the broader BFS-seeded frontier set becomes the primary hypothesis.

Why does c23 (perpendicular advance) have reinits=3 — identical to the no-patch baseline — while c22 shows reinits=4 and c20 shows reinits=5? If the perpendicular advance fires before the 'all floors explored' stop guard rather than resetting reinit_flag after the stop, it may consume fewer floor-reinit events while still extending exploration.

What is the spatial footprint of the floor-2 BFS seed from the stair landing vs the total navigable area on floor 2 in mL8ThkuaVTM? C20 and c23 both reach [3.88535534, -0.08535534] at distances 2.1–2.5m from the dead-end frontier, suggesting the explored area covers roughly a 5–6m radius from the landing; the toilet's DTG≈4.18–4.71 from this region implies it may be in an entirely disconnected corridor segment that would only be exposed by full BFS re-seeding.

bxsVRursffK.root_cause_summary: Success in this 3-floor scene (bed on floor 3) requires two sequential stair climbs and is sensitive to both DP9 and DP10. With DP9=1.2m and DP10='default' (candidates 10, 12, 13, 14), the first stair climb completes at step 159 and the second upward staircase is detected at floor_step=13 (step 172), enabling the second climb and bed discovery at step ~200. Candidate_15 reveals DP10='replace' causes regression to FAIL: the overwrite fusion triggers the first stair climb 14 steps earlier (step 145), placing the agent at a different floor-2 landing position that does not detect the second staircase within the 13-step exploration window; the same 'no frontiers, all floors explored' premature-exhaustion termination pattern as mL8ThkuaVTM fires at step 187. The mechanism is identical across both failures: narrow floor-2 frontier visibility window that is sensitive to landing position.
key_evidence: candidates 10/12/13/14 (SUCCESS): first climb at step 159 (floor_step=0), 'Environment 0: Navigating upstairs to unexplored floor' at step 172 (floor_step=13), second climb at step 180 (floor_step=21), 'climb stair success!!!!' at step 187 (floor_step=0), bed found at step 200 (floor_step=13) — bit-for-bit identical successful trajectory across 4 candidates with DP9=1.2m and DP10='default'

candidate_15 (FAIL): DP10='replace' with DP9=1.2m retained; first climb at step 145 (14 steps earlier than c10–14); floor_step=0 at step 161; floor_step=0 at step 174 (no second stair detected, no-frontier condition fires); floor_step=1 at step 175 (reinit); STOP at step 187 (floor_step=13) — premature-exhaustion termination identical to mL8ThkuaVTM
candidate_11 (FAIL): DP9=0.8m, DP12=100; first climb at step 154; same 180-step no-frontier termination — confirms DP9=1.2m is necessary for placing agent within second-stair detection range

dp7_empty=5/5 in candidate_15 vs 2/2 in candidate_10 — DP10='replace' generates 3 extra LLM triggers before stair climb, reflecting altered frontier value distribution from overwrite vs weighted-average accumulation

min_dis_to_upstair=330 at step 172–173 in successful candidates, decreasing to 324 by step 179 — second staircase detected and approach initiated; this detection event NEVER occurs in candidate_15 after the earlier-timed first climb at step 145

candidate_outcomes.candidate_4: DP12=35, DP3=65: climb success at step 154, stop at step 180 after 13 steps on floor 2
candidate_outcomes.candidate_5: Identical to candidate_4: same trajectory and stop
candidate_outcomes.candidate_6: DP2=always-True, DP5 CoT: same 180-step failure with climb at step 137
candidate_outcomes.candidate_7: DP5+DP6 CoT: same 180-step failure with climb at step 137
candidate_outcomes.candidate_10: DP9=1.2m, DP10='default': SUCCESS (steps=217); first climb step 159, second stair detected at floor_step=13, second climb step 180, bed found step 200 — DP9=1.2m is the confirmed lever; DP10='default' is the necessary companion
candidate_outcomes.candidate_11: DP9=0.8m (baseline), DP12=100: FAIL (steps=180) — same no-frontier termination as baseline; DP12 irrelevant since 'no frontiers' path bypasses it; confirms DP9=1.2m is necessary
candidate_outcomes.candidate_12: DP9=1.2m, DP4=0.65, DP10='default': SUCCESS (steps=217) — identical to candidate_10; DP4 change does not affect stair climb timing or floor-2 landing position
candidate_outcomes.candidate_13: DP9=1.2m, DP10='default': SUCCESS (steps=217) — identical; DP9=1.2m with default value map fusion reliably produces correct landing position across three independent candidates
candidate_outcomes.candidate_14: DP9=1.2m, DP1 smooth bonus, DP10='default': SUCCESS (steps=217) — DP1 change does not affect stair climb timing; default fusion preserves correct landing position
candidate_outcomes.candidate_15: DP9=1.2m, DP10='replace': FAIL (steps=187) — REGRESSION; 'replace' fusion causes first climb 14 steps earlier (step 145 vs 159), floor-2 landing position misses second staircase within 13-step window; floor_step=0 at step 174 (no-frontier reinit), STOP at step 187 (floor_step=13); dp7_empty=5/5 vs 2/2 in c10; DP10='replace' is confirmed harmful for this scene

ruled_out_reasoning.DP12: candidate_11 DP12=100: identical 180-step failure; 'no frontiers' termination bypasses DP12 entirely
ruled_out_reasoning.DP3: DP3=65 in candidates 4/5 had no effect on climb timing or floor 2 outcome
ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: CoT prompt changes in candidates 6/7 had no effect on stair traversal or post-climb exploration
ruled_out_reasoning.DP2: always-True LLM trigger in candidate_6 did not change the stair-based failure mode
ruled_out_reasoning.DP10: candidate_15 'replace' variant REGRESSES this scene from SUCCESS to FAIL; earlier first climb (step 145 vs 159) places agent at floor-2 landing position outside second-staircase detection range within 13-step window; DP10='replace' is confirmed harmful and must not be combined with DP9=1.2m; DP10='default' is load-bearing for this scene's success

highest_leverage_untested_levers: DP9=1.2m with DP10='default' is the confirmed winning configuration for this scene — must be preserved in all future candidates; DP10='replace' is confirmed harmful and must not be re-applied

Track 2 fix to floor-2 frontier seeding after stair climb (ascent_policy.py): the 13-step detection window is narrow and sensitive to landing position; re-running full BFS frontier expansion from all navigable cells after successful stair climb (rather than narrow landing-footprint seeding) would make second-staircase detection robust to the landing position variance induced by DP changes — this fix would simultaneously address mL8ThkuaVTM's identical exhaustion mechanism

Minimum floor exploration guard (Track 2 / ascent_policy.py): the same 13-step exhaustion pattern fires in mL8ThkuaVTM (always) and bxsVRursffK under DP10='replace'; a min_floor_steps guard preventing 'all floors explored' termination until floor_step exceeds threshold would protect both scenes against premature-exhaustion regressions caused by future DP changes that shift landing position
open_questions: Why does DP10='replace' cause the first stair climb 14 steps earlier (step 145 vs 159) — does the overwrite fusion create higher Mss confidence for stair-area frontier cells sooner, triggering passive upstairs detection at floor_step=145 instead of 159? Comparing the value map state at floor_step=145 under 'replace' vs 'default' would confirm this.

Is the second staircase in bxsVRursffK only visible from within a specific ~1m radius on floor 2 that is reached by the landing position at step 159 (c10 carrot) but not step 161 (c15 carrot, 14-step-earlier climb)? The 2-step difference in landing step and the floor_step=13 detection boundary suggest the detection range is extremely narrow.

Can the Track 2 frontier re-seeding fix for mL8ThkuaVTM simultaneously fix the landing-position sensitivity for bxsVRursffK, or is the bxsVRursffK failure specifically about the second staircase falling outside the agent's field of view at the c15 landing position regardless of frontier pool size?

dp7_empty=5/5 in candidate_15 vs 2/2 in candidate_10: are the 3 extra LLM triggers in candidate_15 responsible for a different floor-1 exploration path that alters the position from which passive stair detection fires, or does the different climb timing arise purely from DP10='replace' changing frontier ranking to prioritize the stair-adjacent cell earlier?
